page: The Microsoft Azure DS4 is a virtual machine instance from the DS family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 8 dedicated physical cores and 8 vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL x86_64 processor operating at 2.6 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 28.0 GB of system memory, providing 3.5 GB of RAM per core, along with 57 GB of local storage. The CPU architecture supports advanced instruction sets, including AVX, AVX-512, AES cryptography, and FMA, backed by a 35 MB L3 cache.

Benchmark data indicates a clear performance profile characterized by average multi-core capabilities and weaker single-core execution. Single-core benchmarks, such as stress-ng single-core CPU performance and Geekbench single-core scores, fall into the bottom 25% tier. Similarly, single-threaded tasks like gzip compression and bzip3 decompression show weaker performance. Conversely, multi-threaded operations, including stress-ng multi-core performance, Geekbench multi-core scores, and Passmark CPU tests, consistently rank in the middle 50% tier. Memory bandwidth tests also demonstrate average performance, with read-only small block cached bandwidth reaching 720.11 GB/sec and large block uncached write bandwidth at 43.54 GB/sec.

With a balanced resource allocation of 3.5 GB of memory per core and dedicated CPU resources, the DS4 offers a stable environment for general-purpose workloads. It is suited for multi-threaded applications, software compilation, database operations, and static web serving, where it delivers average throughput. However, the instance exhibits a performance tradeoff, as workloads heavily reliant on single-threaded execution or high-speed single-core throughput will experience limitations. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the instance provides a balanced, mid-tier resource density suitable for standard enterprise applications that do not require specialized GPU acceleration or high-ratio memory configurations.
description: The Microsoft Azure DS4 is an x86_64 virtual machine instance from the DS family, powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor running at 2.6 GHz. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs, 28.0 GB of system memory (3.5 GB per core), and 57 GB of local storage, managed via the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. Performance benchmarks reveal a distinct tradeoff: single-core execution, including single-threaded compression and Geekbench single-core tests, falls into the bottom 25% tier, whereas multi-core operations, memory bandwidth, and database operations consistently perform in the average middle 50% tier. Lacking GPU acceleration, this instance is designed for balanced, general-purpose workloads. It is best suited for multi-threaded applications, software compilation, static web serving, and standard database operations that benefit from dedicated CPU allocation and moderate memory density without requiring high single-core clock speeds.
og_description: Microsoft Azure DS4 is an x86_64 virtual machine featuring 8 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 28 GB of RAM, and 57 GB of local storage, delivering average multi-core performance for general-purpose workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure DS4 is an x86_64 virtual machine with 8 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 28 GB RAM, and 57 GB local storage, optimized for general-purpose workloads.
tagline: A balanced x86_64 virtual machine with dedicated Intel Xeon cores, offering stable multi-threaded performance and local storage for standard enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 8 dedicated Intel Xeon 8272CL vCPUs at 2.6 GHz
28.0 GB of system memory and 57 GB of local storage
Average multi-core and memory bandwidth benchmark performance
Weaker single-core and single-threaded execution speeds
Well-suited for multi-threaded applications, software compilation, and database operations
